curation_notes: Two patch commits are referenced: b5a8f93b87720d5d5f033a4b3a131ce0fb621dbc (adds password recovery methods with proper hashing to UserManager.php) and e1285709f6f6a2277bd0075acf369f33cefd78f7 (updates LostPasswordAction.php to use the new methods and removes the hard-coded salt and MD5). The pre-patch commit (7bfc671) is the parent of the first patch. In the pre-patch code, the vulnerable sendPasswordRecoveryEmail function in LostPasswordAction.php uses MD5 with a hard-coded salt (PW_SALT = 'FBcA') and a small random integer (0-10000) to generate password recovery keys. The advisory description attributes the code to UserManager.php, but at the pre-patch commit the vulnerable code is only in LostPasswordAction.php. No sibling files share the same vulnerability pattern. Vulnerability class crypto-weakness confirmed from CWE-327 and patch diff.
